ops.title: codex 0.17.0 disk-truth result protocol — decision + experiment verdicts
ops.body:
Shipped codex 0.17.0 (cc-skills e2a10de484f3, CI green; guides re-render 18aff7b3). Addresses the wrap-up incident "codex died after writing files, before its reply → unverified tree" plus two follow-ups (get 0.17.0 live; explore structured outputs).

## Decision: the disk is the record, narration is advisory
A delegated agent's final message is lossy; the deliverable lives at a caller-chosen disk address. codex-ask already persists meta/status/reply/log per scratch dir, mv-atomic, before any narration. New surface:
- `--collect <root>`: read-only, classifies each fan-out lane from disk alone as JSONL (no-run/pending/running/died/completed/failed). Roster-visible: a minted-but-never-dispatched lane reads no-run, not absent.
- Staged reply: codex writes `-o "$R.tmp"`, published to `$R` only on `rc==0 && -s`, before the status write. Invariant: non-empty `$R` ⇔ codex exited 0 with a complete reply.
- poll_status recovery: dead pid + no status + non-empty `$R` → synthesize durable status=0 (a killed-but-finished run is recovered, not just detected).
- report_status marker: status 0 + `turn.started` present but no `turn.completed` → "died mid-turn, tree may hold partial unverified edits" (belt over the exit code; the empty-reply guard remains primary).
- reuse wipe now removes meta too ("meta present ⇒ latest launch attempt"; no meta ⇒ safe redo).

## C1 verdict — SHIP `--schema` (verdict lanes only)
Live smoke on gpt-5.6-sol / codex-cli 0.144.5: `--output-schema` returned a conforming object, NO model-family guard (#4181 does not bite sol), no leak (#19816 unobserved), clean turn.started+turn.completed. Shipped opt-in `codex-ask --schema FILE` (→ --output-schema). Verdict lanes carry `{status,summary}` via --schema; impl lanes stay schema-free (a schema after a long session risks the StructuredOutput retry cap nulling finished work — Incident 2).

## C3 verdict — NO impl-lane wrapper envelope
Evidence-based: (a) native --schema already covers verdict lanes cleanly (C1), so an envelope adds nothing there; (b) for impl lanes the envelope's completed/tree_touched fields are strictly less reliable than disk (a dead agent self-reports nothing; disk still has the truth) and it re-imports Incident 2's retry-cap failure. The live 2-lane kill/recover smoke shows disk+collect recovering where an envelope would null. Keep verbatim reply + REPLY_FILE pointer + --collect. No impl-lane envelope shipped.

## Review (finder gpt-5.6-sol + refuter opus): fixed vs deferred
FIXED: rc-gate on reply mv (a nonzero-exit codex that wrote -o must not publish); chained status/pid tmp writes (&&) so a failed write can't publish a stale tmp; json_escape now escapes LF (0x0A gap); excerpt read no longer wiped on a missing trailing newline; no-run lanes drop the unusable await recipe.
VERIFIED-FALSE (refuter, against real `codex exec --json`): the turn.completed belt does NOT break clean calls (fails OPEN on event rename); narration can't fool the grep (codex JSON-escapes reply text); staged reply can't fake status=0; --collect is read-only/non-blocking.
DEFERRED (residual risks): pid-recycle can mislabel a dead lane `running` / hang --await (misty-lemon sanctioned deferral; a started-timestamp is the fix if it bites); concurrent shared `-s` is sanctioned last-writer-wins (protocol mints unique lane dirs); codex `-o` ENOSPC-with-exit-0 can leave a truncated file codex claims complete (upstream codex, undetectable from bash); turn.completed grep is unanchored (kept for fail-open robustness; an MCP tool-call arg containing the marker is a contrived false-positive).

## Still open
Marketplace/user-scope plugin migration (codex/cc-context/cc-notes/llm-prompts/writing-docs are project-scope; cc-context+cc-notes MCP disconnected mid-session — the exact churn the migration fixes). Fleet fragment removal is fleet-wide + hard-to-reverse; held for a deliberate go/no-go. codex 0.17.0 is on git+main; the live PATH cache is still 0.16.1 until the scope migration or a cache update.
